message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class for CPU priority control

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Extending click.edit() to support editing multiple files via Iterable[str] filename param

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Renamed Editor.edit_file(filename) to Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]); termui.edit() filename param now accepts str|Iterable[str]|None. Please rebase process_priority changes onto edit_files() and its subprocess.Popen call rather than edit_file().

type: ANSWER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: My process_priority change only adds a param to Editor.__init__ and wraps the existing Popen call in edit_file() with priority handling (creationflags on Windows, os.setpriority on Unix after Popen). No rename/signature change to edit_file/filename. If you rename edit_file->edit_files, just apply the same creationflags/setpriority logic to edit_files' Popen call and thread process_priority through Editor.__init__ and termui.edit().

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: My multi-file edit_files() change is finalized and submitted as my own patch; not merging process_priority in since that's your separate assigned feature — go ahead and layer creationflags/setpriority onto edit_files' Popen call in your own patch.
